verdict.caveat: Apply LLM adjudication only to the true residual (~171 rows), not the full 1,599. The majority of ambiguous rows are resolvable deterministically.
verdict.missing_step_in_proposal: Stage A (enhanced deterministic resolver) must precede LLM adjudication. Skipping it wastes inference budget and introduces noise into rows where rules suffice.

signals.name: CU Match
signals.description: SP row has explicit sp_cus; exactly one candidate matches that CU value in canonical_courses.

signals.name: One Active Candidate
signals.description: Exactly one candidate has active_current = True in canonical_courses.

signals.name: A-Suffix Certificate Context
signals.description: One+ candidates carry A-suffix with contexts_seen=cert; guide is a degree program. Non-cert candidate wins.

signals.name: Degree-Level Exclusion
signals.description: Guide is UG or grad. Candidates exclusively scoped to opposite level are excluded. If exactly one remains, it wins.

signals.name: Degree-Title in Current Programs
signals.description: Exactly one candidate has current_programs or historical_programs containing the guide's degree_title_guide as a substring.

true_llm_residual.dominant_pattern_description: One or more candidates have current_programs='' and historical_programs=''. Same CU, same college as the correct candidate. Rules cannot distinguish. LLM can infer from program family context, college alignment, legacy vs active code patterns, and parsed description.

example_cases.title: Elementary Social Studies Methods
example_cases.rule_failure: Degree-level eliminates D681. C104 vs D674 both 3CU same college — only distinguishable by program text and legacy code pattern.

example_cases.title: Calculus I
example_cases.rule_failure: Degree-level removes grad candidates. C958 wrong college. C282/C362 still compete with D890 — distinguished only by program text and CU (3 vs 4) where no SP CU is available on AoS rows.

stage_a.name: Enhanced Deterministic Resolver
stage_a.logic: Apply signals 1-5 in order. First match wins. If multi-signal agreement: record all. If conflict: flag (should not occur based on current data). If no signal: forward as ambiguous_residual.

stage_c.name: LLM Adjudication
stage_c.approach: Process all 171 packets in one or two grouped batches by program family. Group by family to exploit repeated context.

failure_modes.description: LLM assigns high confidence to structurally indistinguishable candidates (same CU, same college, both empty programs).
failure_modes.mitigation: Require at least one named evidence signal in rationale for high-confidence label. If rationale cites only 'seems more likely', downgrade to medium.

failure_modes.description: Some empty-programs codes cannot be resolved because the guide may genuinely intend either version.
failure_modes.mitigation: Treat unresolvable as a valid, expected output. Do not force a selection.

failure_modes.description: AoS rows have no CU or term context. Parsed guide description may not distinguish versions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Always include parsed description AND neighboring resolved titles in AoS packets.

failure_modes.description: Deterministic resolver errors propagate to enrichment output.
failure_modes.mitigation: Spot-check 20 resolved rows before running Stage E. Validate that resolved codes appear in expected program families.

what_stays_deterministic: All rows with existing unique anchor classes (exact_current_unique, exact_observed_variant_unique)
All unmapped rows (no candidates to choose from)
All multi-signal rows where signals agree
Do not use LLM to double-check deterministic resolutions

recommended_next_session.title: Build Enhanced Deterministic Resolver + LLM Packet Builder
recommended_next_session.priority_4: Spot-check sample of deterministic resolutions manually
recommended_next_session.optional: Run 171 LLM adjudication packets in same session if time permits
recommended_next_session.do_not: Rerun enrichment extraction until both deterministic and LLM stages complete
